Ad Text:
About the Company

Global organisation operating across multiple regions, supporting a large and complex enterprise environment

Well-established leader in its industry with a strong focus on innovation, risk management, and customer outcomes

Collaborative, people-first culture with a clear investment in cyber security maturity and transformation

About the Role
This is a key role within a global cyber security function, focused on driving governance, reporting, and data-led decision making. You'll be responsible for analysing control performance, identifying risk signals, and translating complex cyber data into meaningful insights for senior stakeholders.

The role also plays an important part in cyber scenario modelling - helping define and refine scenarios aligned to threat intelligence and attack methodologies, and embedding these insights into executive-level reporting.

Experience Required

Strong experience in cyber security governance, risk, or control assurance roles

Ability to analyse and interpret security control metrics and risk indicators

Experience with reporting tools such as Excel, Power BI, or similar analytics platforms

Good understanding of security frameworks, risk methodologies, and control environments

Strong stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to communicate complex concepts clearly

Experience working in large, global or multi-region environments

Role Accountabilities

Monitor and analyse control performance metrics and KRIs to identify trends and emerging risks

Develop dashboards, reporting packs, and governance materials for senior stakeholders

Support cyber scenario modelling activities, ensuring outputs are relevant and defensible

Collaborate with control owners and delivery teams to improve data quality and reporting consistency

Contribute to assurance activities and continuous improvement of governance frameworks

Benefits

Hybrid / WFH working model to support work-life balance

Generous parental leave offering, inclusive of superannuation

Opportunity to work in a global, high-performing cyber security team with strong career progression opportunities

The Opportunity
  
This is an opportunity to join a well-regarded Direct Equities team and contribute to Mutual Trust’s international public equities capability in a meaningful way.
  
Working closely with the International Equities Portfolio Manager and the broader Direct Equities team, you will play a key role in international public equities research, stock selection and investment insight through a disciplined, fundamentally driven investment process.
  
This role will suit an experienced equities analyst with strong valuation capability, sound commercial judgement and a genuine passion for markets. It will also appeal to someone who is open to evolving research practices through the thoughtful use of technology, structured analytics and AI-enabled tools, while remaining grounded in fundamental company analysis and investment judgement.
  
What You’ll Do  

Deliver deep fundamental research across international equities, generating high-conviction investment ideas and clear recommendations
Work closely with the Portfolio Manager to support portfolio decisions, challenge thinking and add genuine investment value
Build and maintain robust financial models, valuation work and company analysis across global markets
Bring structure, rigour and curiosity to the research process, contributing to the team’s high standards of insight
Contribute to the development a modern research environment through the thoughtful use of data, technology and AI-enabled tools
Translate complex market and company information into clear insights and high-quality client-facing content
  
About You
You are a high-calibre equities analyst with intellectual curiosity, sound commercial judgement and a genuine passion for investing. You enjoy deep research, think independently and bring the discipline, ownership and rigour needed to produce high-quality investment insight in a sophisticated environment.
  
You bring:

5+ years’ experience in international and/or Australian public equities research
Well-developed fundamental research, financial modelling and valuation capability
A disciplined approach and the ability to produce timely, thorough and well-reasoned analysis
Genuine curiosity, excellent work ethic and high levels of ownership and accountability
Familiarity with quantitative frameworks, screening tools or structured approaches that support stock research and portfolio monitoring
An openness to technology, data and AI-enabled research tools, with the judgement to apply them appropriately within a research-led, fundamental investing environment
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
A collaborative approach and the ability to build strong relationships across the business
Tertiary qualifications in Finance, Accounting, Commerce or a related discipline
CFA, MBA or Master of Finance qualifications will be highly regarded
  
Culture & Benefits
Joining Mutual Trust offers you the opportunity to enjoy:

Supportive flexible work arrangements.
Opportunity to purchase up to four weeks’ additional leave each year.
Inclusive and equitable culture - proudly recognised as an Employer of Choice for Gender Equality (Workplace Gender Equality Agency).
Award winning industry leadership - winner of the Euromoney Private Banking Awards 2026 - Australia’s Best for Family Office Services.
Professional training and development for staff of all levels.
Yearly health and well-being allowance and ongoing activities.
Employee assistance program that offers free counselling and support for you and your family.
Annual performance payment (based on your performance and Firm-wide results).
Paid community/volunteer days and an award-winning staff giving program.
Reward and Recognition program.
Ongoing staff entertainment.
About Us
Mutual Trust is a Multi-Family Office which provides integrated wealth services to some of Australia’s most successful families, family businesses, trustees, not-for-profits and individuals. Our purpose is to help families achieve what matters most.

About Our Team
Mutual Trust has over 250 staff, located across our offices in Melbourne, Sydney, Perth and Adelaide. Our team enjoys a fun and engaging culture. We have diverse roles and opportunities across, wealth management, family office advisory, accounting, tax, agri-business, trustee and philanthropy competencies, supported by a broad corporate team of legal, financial, marketing and P&C professionals.

How to Apply
Submit your application online with the following:

A cover letter, and
A detailed resume in Word format.
For more information on this role, call Kate Farmer on 03 9605 9645.
Only applicants with permanent residency or without visa restrictions will be considered.

Job Title: Business Analyst / Project Manager - GRC Technology Project
Ad Text:
An organisation supporting the rollout of major renewable energy infrastructure across NSW is seeking a Business Analyst / Project Manager to join their Risk & Compliance team on a fixed-term basis (~ 12 weeks with potential extension).

You will lead a focused project to define business requirements and evaluate Governance, Risk and Compliance (GRC) systems, helping uplift risk and compliance capabilities in a high-impact environment.

This opportunity offers the chance to contribute to the rollout of renewable energy infrastructure across NSW while leading a high-impact project to evaluate and select a Governance, Risk and Compliance platform, working closely with risk, compliance and senior stakeholders in a visible and meaningful role.

The Role
Working closely with the Risk & Compliance team, you will operate as a standalone contributor, owning delivery of a structured technology evaluation project.

Key responsibilities include:

Translating business needs into a Business Requirements Document (BRD)
Documenting functional and non-functional system requirements
Assessing Governance, Risk and Compliance (GRC) platforms
Coordinating vendor engagement and supporting EOI and RFP processes
Preparing presentations and recommendations for stakeholders
Managing the project plan, RAID log, meetings and deliverables
Supporting development of a risk management framework
If extended, you may also support implementation of the selected platform, including user acceptance testing and go-live.

About You
You are a hands-on Business Analyst or Project Manager comfortable working independently and delivering outcomes in a small team environment.

You will bring:

Experience as a Business Analyst, Project Manager or BA/PM hybrid
Strong experience preparing Business Requirements Documents (BRDs)
Experience supporting technology evaluation, vendor selection or procurement processes
Exposure to risk, compliance, governance or regulatory environments
Ability to manage small projects end-to-end as a sole contributor
Strong stakeholder engagement, communication and presentation skills
Experience with GRC or risk management platforms (e.g. Protecht, ServiceNow or similar systems) will be advantageous.

Apply
If you are a Business Analyst or Project Manager with experience delivering risk, compliance or technology projects, we’d welcome your application.

Apply now or contact Pack Heng (pack@hrg.com.au) for a confidential discussion.

HRG is a specialist recruitment and advisory firm supporting clients across technology, infrastructure and renewable energy sectors.

Reporting to the Head of Risk & Compliance, this position is accountable for managing operational risks arising from operations within the Enterprise Services division. The Operational Risk Lead is responsible for ensuring the effectiveness, compliance, and integrity of activities across the Customer Care, Technology, and Marketing business units. This role involves executing risk management and assurance activities, ensuring adherence to company policies, legal obligations, and industry standards.

 

Let’s look at some key responsibilities:


Ensure adherence to Enterprise Risk Policies and frameworks by undertaking assurance activities and providing valuable insights and trends to appropriate governance forums.

Ensure effective maintenance of risk registers and profiles, including risk identification, execution of RCSA’s and resolution of self-identified, internal, and external audit issues.

Partner with Senior Managers to ensure business risks are identified and managed. Where risks have materialised (risk events/incidents), ensure they are captured and remediated efficiently, including conducting thorough root cause analysis.

Undertake independent testing of control portfolios to ensure compliance with relevant obligations and industry standards. Partner with stakeholders to address control gaps and implement operational efficiencies.

Provide high-quality risk reporting to business leadership and risk committees.

Drive continuous strategic initiatives that strengthen and enhance the risk management position of the Enterprise Services division, including problem resolution, event root cause remediation, and operational improvements.

Conduct detailed risk assessments and update frameworks and relevant policies and procedures to support regulatory/ process changes, outsourcing arrangements, and technology or business initiatives.

Develop and deliver the Line 1 assurance plan, ensuring alignment with higher-risk areas and regulatory obligations.

Establish and monitor Key Risk Indicators (KRIs); analyse trends and escalate breaches promptly.

Provide high-quality risk reporting to business leadership and risk committees.

Lead proactive assessments to identify process improvement opportunities, including streamlining, automation, and the design or enhancement of controls to strengthen resilience and efficiency.

Perform any other duties as may be reasonably required from time to time.




 We're looking for the following: 


Relevant tertiary qualifications or demonstrated equivalent knowledge through prior work experience.

5+ years’ experience in Operational Risk Management, Internal Audit, or Quality Assurance practices and programs.

Technology, Operations and Marketing experience banking or financial services environment, with a solid understanding of core processes workflows and customer journeys within the financial services industry.

Strong understanding of regulatory frameworks and compliance obligations relevant to consumer finance / financial services across Australia and New Zealand. 

Demonstrated expertise in control design, testing, and uplift initiatives including obligation registers and mapping.

Experience leading assurance reviews and thematic risk assessments to identify and address systemic issues.

Strong analytical, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills.

Excellent communication and report writing skills, with an ability to translate technical issues into business-friendly language.

Proficiency in interpreting complex process maps, data flows, system controls, and operational workflows.

Strong ability to collaborate with cross-functional teams, with excellent communication skills and the capability to influence and articulate complex risk scenarios.

Relevant Operational Risk Management experience within the Australian financial services industry.

Knowledge of, and experience with, risk management systems, including Protecht.







Sometimes the best candidates don’t have 100% of what is listed above, but if you have most and are confident, you’d be a good fit, we’d love to hear from you!

Ad Text:
Senior Leadership opportunity shaping the future of the Australian economy

Lead a new econometrics unit applying cutting‑edge time series methods to understand economic shocks, macro‑financial dynamics, and policy counterfactuals.

Play a pivotal role in strengthening policy insights that support the wellbeing of all Australians.



About the Department

The Reserve Bank of Australia's (RBA) Economic Research Department plays a pivotal role in deepening the Bank and Board's understanding of the economy and the evolving impact of monetary policy.

We are seeking strategic leader to lead a new unit on time series econometrics research, building cutting‑edge analytical capability to inform research and policy.

About the Role

As part of the senior leadership team in the Economic Research department, you will establish and lead a new macro-­finance econometrics unit specialising in advanced time‑series techniques used to understand economic shock identification, propagation and macro‑financial dynamics. This unit will complement the Bank's large macro models and provide robust, flexible tools for analysing macroeconomic shocks and constructing policy counterfactuals.

What You'll Do


Lead strategy, delivery and capability uplift across in time series econometrics and empirical macro-financial economics.

Build, develop and mentor high‑performing teams grounded in innovation and technical excellence.

Develop complementary analytical insights to support monetary policy, risk assessment and scenario design.

Collaborate with economists across the policy departments.

Communicate complex analysis clearly and effectively to senior decision‑makers.

About You


Proven leadership in time series econometrics in policy‑relevant settings.

Strong technical expertise and a background of delivering innovative work in time series econometrics, including academic publication in leading econometrics journals.

Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills.

Experience in central banking, macroeconomics or financial markets is highly valued.

PhD in econometrics or a related field.

Be More

Working for an organisation that truly makes a difference to the people of Australia, we can offer development and career opportunities in a collaborative environment that supports your growth, wellbeing and promotes flexibility. Your individual growth and success drives the RBA forward as an organisation. Be more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

Why RBA?

The RBA makes an important contribution to the Australian economy through the pursuit of national economic policy objectives and associated activities in financial markets and banking. We also issue Australia's banknotes and operate infrastructure critical to the payments system, all of which contribute to the welfare of the Australian people. Made up of specialists across a wide range of fields, our people, values, and culture play a critical role in achieving our objectives. Striving to be Open & Dynamic, we consider and incorporate different perspectives, work across teams and are transparent with each other, whilst delivering quality together effectively and focusing on outcomes by prioritising, testing, learning, and refining as we go. Our people conduct themselves with a high degree of integrity, while striving for excellence in the work they perform and the outcomes they achieve. We encourage intelligent inquiry and we treat one another with respect while promoting the public interest through our efforts. We know it is the growth and success of our people that drives the RBA forward. Come and make a bigger contribution while you build and develop your own skills too, because being more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

The Reserve Bank of Australia is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion through key initiatives. We welcome and encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, culturally and linguistically diverse background, those living with a disability and from the LGBTQ+ community. We are committed to making the recruitment process fair and equitable for all our candidates.

Seeking an experienced, senior and influential Delivery/Scrum Lead to support our Higher Education client

Your new company


A large, complex and purpose-driven organisation at the forefront of education, research and digital transformation. The organisation is undertaking a long-term digital strategy to modernise its technology ecosystem and enhance experiences for staff, students and stakeholders. You will be joining a mature IT environment that values collaboration, modern delivery practices and continuous improvement.




Your new role


As a Delivery Lead, you will play a senior leadership role coordinating scaled delivery across multiple cross-functional squads operating within a Learning & Development value stream. Acting as a servant leader, you will connect strategy through to execution, ensuring delivery is aligned to prioritised business outcomes.

You will work closely with Product Owners, Value Stream leadership, Chapter Leads and Scrum Masters to plan and coordinate work, remove impediments, manage dependencies and uplift delivery maturity. The role liaises extensively across the wider IT structure, including Delivery and Change Chapters, as well as multiple multidisciplinary squads.

Key responsibilities include:
- Coordinating scaled Agile delivery across several squads within a value stream
- Leading effective planning, resourcing and delivery governance
- Identifying, managing and escalating risks, issues and dependencies
- Supporting budget planning and financial reporting for delivery of initiatives.
- Driving continuous improvement in Lean and Agile delivery practices
- Coaching and mentoring delivery practitioners to uplift capability
- Promoting a psychologically safe, innovative and high-performing delivery culture

Canberra is the preferred location for this role.




What you'll need to succeed


To be successful, you will bring a strong background in large-scale IT delivery within complex environments, combined with a deep understanding of Agile and Lean delivery approaches.

You will ideally have:
- Extensive experience in senior delivery, program or portfolio leadership roles
- Proven experience leading delivery across multiple Agile squads and stakeholders
- Strong knowledge of business agility frameworks and scaled Agile approaches
- Demonstrated success in driving organisational change and delivering improvement initiatives
- The ability to work collaboratively across organisational boundaries and disciplines
- Highly developed leadership, communication and stakeholder engagement skills
- Experience managing competing priorities, budgets, timelines and outcomes

Relevant postgraduate qualifications, Agile certifications or equivalent experience will be well regarded.




What you'll get in return


In return, you'll be offered:
- A senior, high-impact role in a large-scale digital transformation program


- An initial 6-mth contract with an additional 6-mth extension option
- An Attractive Hourly Rate - $125-145/hr + super for the right candidate

- The opportunity to influence delivery practices at a value-stream and enterprise level
- A collaborative, values-led culture focused on innovation and continuous improvement.
- Work that is meaningful and contributes to long-term organisational outcomes
- Competitive remuneration aligned to senior leadership responsibilities
- Flexibility and support to do your best work (with Canberra as the preferred base)




What you need to do now

If you're interested in this role, please contact Michael Minns via email - Michael.Minns@hays.com.au with an updated copy of your CV tailored to the role, and any availability for a further conversation.

In Australia, the J.R. Simplot Company's core operations are food manufacturing and agriculture, encompassing potato, vegetable, seafood and sauce operations. We supply a wide range of frozen, shelf-stable and chilled products to major retail and foodservice customers in Australia and New Zealand. The Simplot Global Food business includes 17 food manufacturing and processing sites, serving retail and foodservice customers in 37 countries, with 8,000 people across North America, Latin America and Asia Pacific. Globally, the J.R. Simplot Company employs 15,000 people, driving innovation and excellence across diverse industries.

Within the Australian market, we are a leading provider of quality, nourishing and sustainable food, focussed on creating meals that maximises value from the best Australian-grown produce and global seafood. Our iconic brands have been an integral part of Aussie life for generations, and include Leggo's, Birds Eye, Edgell, John West and Chiko.



Culture and Benefits

At Simplot, we are fuelled by our purpose: We Contribute to Feeding Our World. We imagine a better future and set goals to reach it. We are committed to working as one Simplot and are guided by our shared values:

Respect for Resources
Spirit of Innovation
Passion for People

About our role

The Finance Business Partner - Category is a key strategic role responsible for shaping, influencing, and executing the commercial strategy across major market leading product categories. Working closely with Category Directors, this role will support managing the end to end category P&Ls, commercial insight, and strategic challenge to drive improved profitability and long term value creation.


You will lead and support high impact initiatives including long range financial modelling, scenario analysis, and development of major capital investment business cases. The role also plays an important part in influencing the APAC Commercial leadership team through clear, insightful, and strategically aligned financial recommendations. Simplot's vertically integrated business model, from farm through to major retail customers such as Coles and Woolworths, offers unique commercial exposure and the opportunity to influence decisions across the entire value chain.

What you will do

Partner directly with Category Directors to manage the full P&L, drive commercial priorities, and influence strategic decision making.
Lead development of long range financial models, ambition planning, and scenario analysis to support category strategy and profitability improvement.
Evaluate large scale capital investment business cases, including post implementation reviews, with clear articulation of assumptions, risks, and financial outcomes.
Build deep, trusted relationships across the organisation—particularly with Finance teams in Supply Chain, Operations, Marketing, Category, Revenue Growth Management, and Commercial Finance.
This requires a strong "enterprise mindset," proactively leveraging cross functional expertise and resources to drive outcomes.
Deliver compelling, executive ready presentations, distilling complex data into clear insights that influence senior leaders across APAC.
Champion continuous improvement, challenging the status quo and enhancing financial processes, modelling, reporting, and decision support capability.
About you

You bring a highly strategic, commercial, and analytical mindset, with strong curiosity and a proactive approach to finding opportunities and solving problems. You also bring:

Strong financial modelling capability (Excel/Power BI) with the ability to transform and interpret complex data.
A hypothesis led approach to problem solving and structured thinking.
Outstanding communication and presentation skills, including high impact PowerPoint storytelling and executive level presence.
The confidence to challenge, influence, and partner with Director level stakeholders as a trusted advisor.
Resilience and composure under pressure, with the ability to manage tight deadlines and diverse stakeholder personalities.
Independence and initiative, with a proactive, self starting approach to identifying opportunities and driving work forward.
A continuous improvement mindset, always seeking better ways of working, lifting capability, and improving performance.

Job Title: Director, Education and Workforce Policy, Asia
Ad Text:
Overview


We are looking for a Director, Education and Workforce Policy focused on the Asia region to advance policy and translate it into practice to support students, teachers, families, and workers in the age of AI.

This person will join the Global Education and Workforce Policy team in Microsoft Elevate to steer impactful initiatives across the Asia region. Acting as a key external representative for Microsoft, you’ll engage with regional and national officials, build meaningful relationships, and champion strategies that reflect Microsoft’s core values while advancing important policy and business goals. You will help guide Microsoft’s positions on critical education and workforce topics—such as the benefits and risks of AI, the impact of AI on work, responsible AI adoption, and how to ensure all citizens develop foundational AI literacy.  

As Director, Education and Workforce Policy, you will collaborate closely with Microsoft Global Affairs in the Asia region, Elevate AI Skills Directors, and Microsoft’s sales organization to drive policy and the adoption of Microsoft’s products, services, and solutions among government and education clients. This high-impact position also interacts with senior Microsoft leaders and executives, as well as a broad range of internal and external audiences, amplifying Microsoft’s voice and advancing strategic goals. This role reports to the General Manager, Global Education and Workforce Policy.   

Microsoft’s mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. As employees we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others, and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond. 

Responsibilities

Strategy & Program Leadership 

Execute the global education and workforce policy strategy through high-impact advocacy and programs in the Asia region, especially in target countries and localities.

Lead the design and execution of key policy campaigns, collaborating across functions regionally and globally to amplify Microsoft’s voice. 

Policy Development & Advocacy 

Lead regional policy analysis and recommendations on a diverse set of education and workforce issues, with a focus on AI.

Localize the global education and workforce policy agenda to a region and country’s needs and political environment.

Support adoption of AI literacy frameworks, standards, and guidance across education systems (K–12, vocational, higher education, and lifelong learning).

Write policy briefs and responses to government requests for input.

Anticipate emerging opportunities and threats and lead the development of forward-thinking strategies to address them. 

Stakeholder Engagement 

Create coalitions or work with a diverse range of partners—from government agencies to think tanks and community leaders—aligning interests and turning stakeholders into advocates for Microsoft’s policy and business goals.

Cultivate and sustain influential relationships with regional institutions, ministry staff, regulators, and opinion leaders, building Microsoft’s reputation as a trusted partner and thought leader.

Convene and influence key stakeholders, including ministries, employers, labor unions, civil society, and workforce intermediaries. 

Strengthen public-private partnerships with government, including contributions to workforce hubs, advisory councils, and national digital skills initiatives. 

Subject Matter Expertise & Influence 

Advise and collaborate with leaders to secure buy-in on policy positions and prepare leaders for public testimony on critical and complex issues.

Develop national and regional workforce narratives, strategic memos, and policy briefs for senior leadership.  

Prepare executive materials for engagements, external meetings, parliamentary preparation, and strategy sessions.  

Author policy analysis and feedback that summarizes and shapes national and regional education and workforce ecosystems.

Promote responsible AI in education principles across internal and external education and workforce programs. 

Internal Collaboration 

Partner with regional and local Microsoft teams by providing policy analysis, insight briefs, recommendations, and meeting preparation.  

Engage regularly with regional government affairs teams to align country-level actions with global priorities.   

Lead and collaborate on high-impact, cross-group projects to develop actionable, scalable solutions for Microsoft’s education and workforce priorities.

Provide thought leadership to overcome regulatory hurdles, partnering closely with the Public Sector and key business leaders to unlock new market opportunities.

Open doors with policymakers, ministries, and institutions to advance Microsoft’s AI capacity-building solutions.  

Coordinate communications across departments to guarantee all stakeholders stay informed and engaged on evolving issues. 

Qualifications

Required Qualifications

Bachelor’s Degree in Public Policy, Education Policy, Technology Policy, or related field AND plus 6+ years of experience in government affairs, corporate affairs, public policy, or government, or related area.

OR equivalent experience. 

A track record of building and managing strategic relationships to champion education and/or workforce policy priorities.

Demonstrated expertise in AI literacy, responsible AI in education, or digital skills policy.

Preferred Qualifications

8+ years of experience working with government ministries, regional/global institutions, unions, or large cross-sector alliances.   

Familiarity with emerging technology policy issues, or the ability to quickly master complex technical and regulatory topics.

Experience crafting responses to legislative and policy processes.

Exceptional political awareness, analytical ability, and government relations expertise.

Strong writing, analysis, communication, and project management skills.

Ability to work independently, manage complex workstreams, and build trust with senior stakeholders.

Ability to navigate ambiguity in a fast-paced environment and contribute across a wide range of policy activities. 

#CELA

This position will be open for a minimum of 5 days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled.

B2B Financial Services Technology | Global Scale | Sydney (Hybrid)

Greenfield opportunity to define a global quantitative capability


Direct influence on product strategy and international expansion


Work with sophisticated institutional clients at scale


A high-growth B2B financial services technology company is appointing a Head of Quantitative in a newly created role to build and lead its next-generation modelling capability.

Operating at the intersection of wealth management, actuarial science and enterprise software, this business partners with tier-one financial institutions across Australia, the UK and Europe to modernise how financial advice is delivered at scale.

This is a rare opportunity to architect stochastic modelling frameworks from first principles, while building and leading a world-class quantitative function with global reach.

The opportunity
The business is at a pivotal inflection point and is poised for continued market traction.

Having successfully deployed deterministic advice models at institutional scale, the next phase of growth requires the introduction of sophisticated stochastic modelling to unlock new markets, new products and materially improved customer outcomes.

As Head of Quantitative, you will:

Define the quantitative strategy


Lead the technical uplift to stochastic modelling


Build and scale a high-performing team


Act as the ultimate authority on model risk, integrity and regulatory alignment
This is a genuine player–coach role, with significant hands-on technical ownership.

What you’ll be responsible for
Architect and deliver stochastic modelling frameworks (Monte Carlo, scenario generation, multi-goal optimisation)


Lead the transition from deterministic Excel-based models to scalable calculation engines


Translate complex regulatory and advice policy requirements into robust quantitative logic


Build, mentor and scale a quantitative team across Australia and global markets


Partner closely with Product and Engineering to productionise models at scale


Engage directly with tier-one institutional clients, actuarial teams and regulators


Own quantitative governance, validation and model risk standards globally
What it will take to make an impact
Deep expertise in stochastic / actuarial / quantitative modelling


Strong hands-on background in tools such as Excel, Python or actuarial modelling platforms


Experience operating in regulated financial services environments (wealth, retirement, insurance or advice technology)


Proven ability to lead technical teams while remaining hands-on


Confidence engaging with C-level stakeholders, institutional clients and regulators


Comfort operating in ambiguity within a high-growth, product-led business


Advanced qualifications in Actuarial Science, Mathematics, Statistics or a related quantitative field are highly regarded.

If you’re motivated by complex problems, real-world impact and global scale, this is a rare leadership opportunity.  Call Michelle Rubinstein at The Onset on 0413 463 043 for a confidential discussion or apply online.

Who are we? 
 
Standards Australia (SA) is the peak standards development organisation in Australia, with a rich history that dates back to 1922. Currently, we are undergoing a significant transformation and growth phase. In recent years, our workforce has doubled, and we now proudly employ over 200 professionals dedicated to helping shape Australia’s future and fulfilling our mission of empowering communities. 
 
Our organisation brings experts together to create national and international standards that serve as essential guidelines for products and services, ultimately benefiting the Australian community. Our vision is to be a global leader in providing trusted solutions that improve life today and tomorrow. 

About the role 

We are seeking a Portfolio Scheduling & Reporting Lead to join our Group Project Management Office (GPMO).

Reporting to the Head of GPMO, this role is responsible for maintaining the organisation’s enterprise project schedule and portfolio reporting. You will provide a consolidated view of project timelines, dependencies, risks and resource demand across strategic initiatives, enabling senior leaders to make informed decisions on delivery priorities.
  
What you’ll do

Maintain the enterprise master schedule across organisational projects
Identify and manage cross-project dependencies, sequencing risks and delivery impacts
Support Project Managers and business teams to develop aligned and realistic project schedules
Design and maintain portfolio reporting dashboards and executive reporting
Provide insights on milestones, resource demand and delivery progress
Partner with Finance to support portfolio budget tracking and forecasting
Provide leadership and oversight to the Portfolio Reporting Analyst
About you

You are an experienced PMO or portfolio professional with strong analytical and scheduling capability.

You will bring:

5+ years’ experience in PMO, portfolio scheduling or project controls roles
Experience managing complex multi-project schedules and portfolio reporting
Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate delivery data into clear insights
Experience using tools such as MS Project, advanced Excel, Power BI, Jira or Confluence
Experience working within enterprise PMO or programme governance environments
Culture & Benefits 

Standards Australia is proud to have been awarded the prestigious Employer of Choice Award for two consecutive years, 2023 and 2024. This recognition highlights our commitment to creating an exceptional workplace and fostering a culture of excellence. Our iCARE values form the basis of all we do - Integrity, Courage, Accountability, Respect, and Excellence. 
 
We offer a range of People Perks that support flexibility, wellbeing, career growth, and recognition - helping you thrive at work and beyond.

What's next? 
 
If Standards Australia sounds like the place for you, please submit your CV outlining your experience and suitability to this role.
Eligible applicants must be Australian Citizens, Permanent Residents or have legal rights to work in Australia. Shortlisted applicants will be contacted within 15 business days of their application. 

At Standards Australia, we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer, and we are committed to fostering a working environment that values diversity, inclusion, and belonging. Our mission is to empower our communities, and in doing so, we strive to create an organisation that genuinely reflects and respects the diversity of the communities we serve; putting people at the heart of all we do. We welcome applications from people of all cultural and linguistic groups, people of all ages and genders, people of Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander background, people with a disability, and people from the LGBTQIA+ community. Company: State Street Services Australia Pty Ltd
Ad Text:
Who we are looking for

Charles River are looking to expand their established Implementation Services Team. The Consultant is responsible for all aspects of implementation for the Charles River IMS.  They will need to become a product expert in a short amount of time and remain up-to-date as product and industry changes require. A Consultant needs to manage the competing demands of the clients to whom the he or she is assigned, operating administratively as well as hands-on.

The ideal candidate will have a mixture of asset management/business knowledge, business analysis, project experience, relationship management and implementation experience. They will be comfortable running a large and complex implementation project, dealing with all the associated issues, either individually or as part of a larger team.

Why this role is important to us

The team you will be joining is a part of Charles River Development (or CRD), that became a part of State Street in 2018. CRD helps create enterprise investment management software solutions for large institutions in the areas of institutional investment, wealth management and hedge funds. Together we have created first open front-to-back platform - State Street Alpha, that was launched in 2019.

Join us if delivering next generation infrastructure, using emerging technologies like AI and Blockchain sounds like a challenge you are up for.

What you will be responsible for

As Principal Consultant - Implementation Services you will be responsible for:

Lead or Co-manage implementation projects for CRD
Providing a single point of contact for a client
Have superior client facing skills and communication skills with the ability to deal with senior level stakeholders/Clients.
Providing on-going communication and evaluation of project status
Interpreting workflow analysis documents and the conversion of these documents into specifications for CR IMS configuration
Providing guidance to customers around best practice implementations and efficient workflow configuration
Undertaking the daily execution of the project plan steps
Ensuring that a client’s needs are understood and documented
Providing proactive leadership in a client’s utilization of CRD products and services
Facilitating conference calls & meetings with other consultants product experts
Communicating pertinent information on upgrades and major releases
Managing training and consulting resource requests
Monitoring and assisting with the issue escalation process
Responding to additional product requests
Managing the co-ordination of work undertaken by CRD’s representatives in other locations
Maintain a general awareness of industry changes/trends

What we value

These skills will help you succeed in this role:
Experience of managing large projects or multiple smaller projects.
Extensive Front Office experience (order management, dealing with Fund Managers/Traders)
Applications experience with Charles River Trading System/Compliance Master or Equivalent (competitor)
Excellent market instrument knowledge - Fixed Income/derivatives etc.
Some technical knowledge, ideally SQL.
Experience of doing a full life cycle implementation is essential
Experience dealing with users or clients from either a technical or business area
Comfortable making presentations covering business, technical or sales
Third level qualification ideally a Technical Bachelor's Degree.
Travel will be required – may be >50% per calendar year
Flexibility with schedule and ability to work beyond regular business hours

Education & Preferred Qualifications

Bachelor’s Degree from a leading university or college in Finance, Business/Mathematical Finance, Economics or Computer Science
An advanced degree or industry certification such as the CFA is a strong plus.

A minimum of 8-10 years of progressively responsible experience in the Financial / Software Services industry (Financial market information systems background preferred)

About State Street


Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success.

We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future.

As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law.

As the Capital & Liquidity Analyst Lead, you will be a key member of the Financial Analytics team within the Finance & Data Division. You will hold Line 1 accountability for capital, liquidity and balance sheet risk management activities and provide forward‑looking insights that support informed decision making across the Bank.

Your key responsibilities will include:

Lead the development and delivery of the Bank’s ICAAP and Recovery Plan, ensuring alignment with APRA prudential standards and regulatory expectations.

Design and deliver enterprise-wide stress testing frameworks, including scenario design, model development and interpretation of outcomes to inform capital planning and strategic decision making.

Present capital and liquidity insights to Executive Committees and the Board, supporting strategic balance sheet decisions.

Develop and maintain liquidity analysis and metrics, including Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR) capability.

Ensuring strong governance, documentation and alignment across capital, liquidity, ALM, FTP and forecasting frameworks.

Providing technical leadership, coaching and knowledge sharing within the Financial Analytics team.

About you

To be successful in this role, you will bring strong technical capability, sound judgement and the ability to influence and engage senior stakeholders.

You will also demonstrate:

Significant experience in financial institutions, with strong knowledge of balance sheet management, capital, liquidity and financial risk management.

Proven experience leading ICAAP, capital management, stress testing or balance sheet risk frameworks within a regulated financial institution.

The ability to design, interpret and explain complex financial models, stress testing outcomes and risk frameworks.

Strong analytical skills, attention to detail and the ability to translate technical analysis into clear insights for non‑technical audiences.

Well developed communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with confidence working with Executives and senior leaders.

A proactive, risk‑aware mindset and commitment to continuous improvement and professional development.

Qualifications

Tertiary qualification in Finance, Business, Economics, Actuarial Studies or a related discipline is essential.

Postgraduate or professional qualifications (CA, CPA, CFA or similar) and relevant industry memberships are desirable.

A workplace for you to be you. 

Come and work with a team that values your unique perspective, contribution, career development and growth. 

Why you’ll love working with us:

People First Bank – our new brand – says exactly what we’re all about: people. We're committed to supporting and growing our team and creating an outstanding work environment.

You’ll have access to financial and lifestyle benefits to support your success and individuality:

Flexibility and hybrid working arrangements.

Extend your salary with discounts on banking, health insurance, and shopping across Australia.

Advance your career with training, study support, and project opportunities.

Competitive leave benefits that empower employees to take time for themselves and loved ones.

Wellbeing support with our Employee Assistance Program, wellbeing leave, mental health app, and mental health ambassadors’ program.

Make a difference with paid volunteering, Workplace Giving, and diverse community initiatives.

Be recognised for your contributions through our peer-driven recognition program.

A bit about People First Bank: 

In March 2023, People’s Choice and Heritage Bank merged to form Heritage and People’s Choice Limited - Australia's leading mutual bank.  We are now on an exciting journey to bring together our products, services, and technology under a single brand - People First Bank.

We have over 700,000 members and 1,900 employees, with dual head offices in Adelaide and Toowoomba, an Australian-based contact centre and branches across five states. 

With a vision ‘To deliver positive change through banking’, People First Bank serves members and contributes to the community, while offering an inclusive, flexible work environment. We’re big enough to make an impact; but agile enough to influence and do interesting work.

As a Senior Business Analyst, you will play a key role in shaping business strategy and delivering enterprise-aligned solutions within the GBIS PMO team. This full-time, 12 month fixed-term opportunity (hybrid, with three days in the office) is ideal for seasoned professionals who excel at solving complex business problems, influencing senior stakeholders, and driving transformative change.

You’ll be part of a collaborative team based in Collins Street, contributing to strategic programs and mentoring other analysts to build organisational capability.

Please note this role is only open to Australian Citizens.




About the role 

Lead requirements gathering for large, complex, multi-stakeholder programs across business and technology domains. 

Develop business cases and define enterprise-level requirements, ensuring full traceability through design, build, testing, and deployment. 

Conduct AS IS and TO BE process analysis across multiple business units using BPMN 2.0 standards. 

Collaborate with architects, solution designers, finance, and operational leaders to shape strategic solutions aligned with organisational and client outcomes. 

Work closely with finance teams to ensure clear understanding of end-to-end finance and commercial processes including procurement, client billing, contract variations, and cost management. 

Act as a trusted advisor to executives, influencing priorities and scope. 

Mentor junior and mid-level analysts, promoting consistency in methods and tools. 

Contribute to governance forums and track benefits realisation linked to BA deliverables. 

Drive alignment of business requirements with enterprise architecture and future-state capabilities. 




Skills and experience 

Strategic thinker with strong analytical and problem-solving skills. 

Must have Australian Citizenship. 

Deep experience in utilities, transport, telecommunications, or other asset-intensive and field-operations industries. 

Must have experience with implementation or optimisation of Field Service Management systems. 

Strong understanding of finance and commercial processes, including procurement, work order management, billing cycles, invoicing, and revenue recognition. 

Advanced facilitation, negotiation, and conflict management abilities. 

Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills, including executive-level relationships. 

Ability to work autonomously while thriving in fast-paced and collaborative environments. 

Leadership and mentoring capability with a focus on building BA practice maturity and consistent analysis standards. 




Experience: 

7+ years in business analysis with proven success in IT environments. 

Extensive experience in business process modelling (BPMN 2.0) and enterprise-level analysis. 

Strong background in ERP or finance-related programs and large-scale implementations. 

Proficiency in tools such as Visio, JIRA, Confluence, and advanced data analysis methods. 

Exposure to Agile frameworks and ability to guide teams in Agile practices. 




About Us 

Service Stream is an equal opportunity ASX-listed business that develops and operates Australia's essential services networks across telecommunications, utilities, transport, defence, and social infrastructure industries. We keep communities connected. 




Benefits of working with us include: 

A safe and supportive culture that values diversity and inclusion. 

Flexible hybrid working arrangements. 

Genuine career development and advancement opportunities. 

Access to special employee benefits and discounts. 

Various paid leave options – parental, cultural, community service, study, corporate volunteering, and purchased leave. 




How to apply 

Please submit your interest with the Apply button where you will be directed to create a profile on our system. We celebrate diversity at Service Stream and welcome applications from Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander peoples, people with a disability or neurodivergence, and those from LGBTIQA+, Veteran or other diverse groups. 
Applications will not be accepted via email. If you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process, please email talent.corporate@servicestream.com.au.

Job Title: Senior Financial Planning and Analytics (Business Partner)
Ad Text:
A senior finance leadership opportunity is available with our client, a large and complex institution operating in the education sector in Canberra. This appointment is being offered as a maternity leave cover and is expected to continue until June 2027. A competitive total remuneration package is available.

This role exists to provide end-to-end financial leadership and commercial support across a broad operating environment. The successful candidate will help shape decision-making by bringing together financial control, planning, modelling and commercial insight. They will work closely with senior stakeholders, translating complex financial information into practical advice that supports both day-to-day operations and longer-term objectives.

The position also carries people leadership responsibility. It will lead a finance team spanning management, analyst and processing capability, while working across a wide stakeholder group to improve performance, strengthen processes and ensure high quality financial support.

What the role will focus on

Leading financial planning, forecasting, reporting and analysis across the function
• Ensuring financial control processes are effective and aligned with broader organisational requirements
• Advising senior leaders on commercial decisions, strategic priorities and emerging risks
• Supporting business case development and objective review with sound commercial judgement
• Building trusted relationships across a diverse stakeholder environment, including external partners
• Strengthening finance service delivery through clear leadership and continuous improvement
What success looks like
The person in this role will provide reliable, well-judged financial and commercial advice in a complex setting. They will support senior stakeholders with clarity and credibility, keep financial operations running effectively and lead a capable team that delivers accurate, timely and practical support.

Required experience and skills

Relevant tertiary qualifications plus CA or CPA, or equivalent
• Extensive senior level experience across financial management, control, commercial management and modelling
• Strong capability in insight driven analysis and turning that analysis into practical recommendations
• Advanced communication skills, with the judgement to adapt messages for different audiences
• Proven stakeholder management experience, including influencing and negotiation at senior level
• Demonstrated leadership capability in complex organisations
• Strong technology skills, including advanced Excel and exposure to enterprise systems
Benefits

Fixed term senior appointment, 12-18 months
• Competitive total remuneration package
Pre employment checks will be required before commencement. The brief refers to standard verification and screening requirements.
To discuss this opportunity confidentially, contact sarah.purdue@bluefinresources.com.au

Category:
Product Management & Development (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - Federal (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
About the ABC 

The ABC is the nation's most trusted and independent source of Australian conversations, culture, and stories. With over 4,000 employees from diverse backgrounds across over 50 locations around Australia and overseas, we are proud to create, curate and deliver high-quality content that informs, educates and entertains Australian communities 

About the Role 

As Product Manager, Audience Reengagement you will be responsible for shaping product strategy and delivery, while driving technology and capability enhancements across key ABC marketing, media and related audience reengagement tools and platforms. 

Working within a cross-functional team you will lead the technical management and optimisation of the ABC’s Marketing Automation Platform and CRM capabilities, within the broader ABC technology ecosystem of data, personalisation, recommendations, identity and audience facing products. You will contribute to roadmap development and be responsible for translating priorities into clear, actionable backlogs and delivery plans.  

You will work closely with Marketing, Content and Data stakeholders to identify opportunities to improve audience engagement and retention, ensuring initiatives are informed by data and aligned to organisational objectives. This includes supporting experimentation, defining success metrics and contributing to performance reporting. You will provide technical subject matter leadership and guidance to ensure platforms achieve privacy, cyber security and technology best practice, while supporting activities around platform monitoring and technical resolution of incidents and issues. 

 In this role you will help ensure strong delivery discipline across planning cycles, maintain attention to detail in platform implementation and support governance and privacy standards. You will also contribute to improving ways of working across product and engineering to ensure sustainable, high-quality outcomes. 

This is an opportunity to work on high-impact platforms that directly shape how millions of Australians experience and engage with the ABC. 

About You 

You are an established product manager with experience working on CRM, marketing automation or audience engagement platforms. You have hands-on familiarity with tools such as Braze (or similar platforms) and understand how lifecycle communications and segmentation strategies drive audience value. 

You bring strong analytical thinking and are comfortable working with data to inform prioritisation and measure impact. You can translate technical or data-related concepts into clear requirements and collaborate effectively with engineering teams. 

You are organised, detail-oriented and capable of managing multiple workstreams. You build positive working relationships across disciplines and communicate clearly with stakeholders who have varying levels of technical understanding. You're someone who is technically minded and comfortable deep in the detail, but you can also collaborate effectively with business stakeholders to drive audience strategy and outcomes. 

Most importantly, you are motivated by using digital platforms and data to strengthen meaningful audience relationships in a public service context. 

For further information on this position please contact Jamie Nedved, Recruitment Lead via email nedved.jamie@abc.net.au   

We respectfully request that Recruitment Agencies do not submit applications for this position. 

If you require recruitment or selection information in an alternative format, or need adjustments or accessibility support, please email accessibility@abc.net.au 

In line with our focus on diversity, applications are strongly encouraged from Indigenous Australians, people from a range of cultural and linguistic backgrounds, people with disability and LGBTIQA+ individuals. The ABC also aims to achieve a gender-balanced workforce 

For more information on working at the ABC visit  abc.net.au/careers

As Product Manager, Audience Reengagement you will be responsible for shaping product strategy and delivery, while driving technology and capability enhancements across key ABC marketing, media and related audience reengagement tools and platforms. 

Working within a cross-functional team you will lead the technical management and optimisation of the ABC’s Marketing Automation Platform and CRM capabilities, within the broader ABC technology ecosystem of data, personalisation, recommendations, identity and audience facing products. You will contribute to roadmap development and be responsible for translating priorities into clear, actionable backlogs and delivery plans.  

You will work closely with Marketing, Content and Data stakeholders to identify opportunities to improve audience engagement and retention, ensuring initiatives are informed by data and aligned to organisational objectives. This includes supporting experimentation, defining success metrics and contributing to performance reporting. You will provide technical subject matter leadership and guidance to ensure platforms achieve privacy, cyber security and technology best practice, while supporting activities around platform monitoring and technical resolution of incidents and issues. 

 In this role you will help ensure strong delivery discipline across planning cycles, maintain attention to detail in platform implementation and support governance and privacy standards. You will also contribute to improving ways of working across product and engineering to ensure sustainable, high-quality outcomes. 

This is an opportunity to work on high-impact platforms that directly shape how millions of Australians experience and engage with the ABC. 

About You 

You are an established product manager with experience working on CRM, marketing automation or audience engagement platforms. You have hands-on familiarity with tools such as Braze (or similar platforms) and understand how lifecycle communications and segmentation strategies drive audience value. 

You bring strong analytical thinking and are comfortable working with data to inform prioritisation and measure impact. You can translate technical or data-related concepts into clear requirements and collaborate effectively with engineering teams. 

You are organised, detail-oriented and capable of managing multiple workstreams. You build positive working relationships across disciplines and communicate clearly with stakeholders who have varying levels of technical understanding. You're someone who is technically minded and comfortable deep in the detail, but you can also collaborate effectively with business stakeholders to drive audience strategy and outcomes. 

Most importantly, you are motivated by using digital platforms and data to strengthen meaningful audience relationships in a public service context.

Company description:



Deloitte Services Pty Ltd



Job description:



Job title: Transformation Lead - ITS Automation CoE

Flexible work arrangements - work in a way that suits you best
Mentoring programs - receive support and coaching to progress your career
Impactful transformation work - help shape how organisations improve and scale their operations

We are looking for an experienced Transformation Lead professional to join our Automation Centre of Excellence in Sydney, driving process transformation and operational excellence across complex organisations. In this role, you'll work closely with business leaders to identify opportunities to standardise, optimise and reimagine key processes, using modern approaches such as process mining and data-led transformation. This is an exciting opportunity to help organisations unlock efficiency, improve performance, and build scalable processes for the future.

What will your typical day look like?

In this role, you'll be working with stakeholders across multiple teams to understand how their processes operate today and where meaningful improvements can be made. You'll combine process analysis, data insights and strong stakeholder engagement to design and deliver transformation initiatives that create measurable value.

Day to day you will be analysing current state processes, identifying opportunities for standardisation and re-engineering, and working alongside teams to implement improved ways of working. You'll also leverage process mining tools and analytics to uncover inefficiencies, validate improvements and ensure benefits are realised. Along the way, you'll help embed new processes by facilitating workshops, building clear documentation, and supporting teams through change.

About the team

You'll be joining a collaborative Automation Centre of Excellence focused on helping organisations transform how work gets done. The team brings together expertise across automation, process improvement and digital transformation to solve complex operational challenges.
Working alongside experienced transformation specialists, you'll contribute to large-scale improvement initiatives and help establish best-practice process frameworks that enable organisations to operate more efficiently and effectively.

Enough about us, let's talk about you.

You may have all or some of the following skills / experiences:

5+ years of experience in process transformation, business process improvement, or operational excellence
Experience driving process standardisation or re-engineering initiatives across complex organisations
Strong knowledge of process mapping, analysis methodologies, and frameworks such as Lean or Six Sigma
Hands-on experience with process mining tools such as Celonis, UiPath Process Mining, Signavio or similar platforms
Experience analysing process performance using data and translating insights into actionable improvements
Proven ability to engage and influence stakeholders across multiple levels of an organisation
Experience facilitating workshops and guiding teams through change and adoption of new processes
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with a focus on delivering measurable business outcomes
Ability to lead cross-functional initiatives and manage competing priorities in a fast-paced environment
Relevant qualifications in Business, Engineering, Information Systems or similar disciplines; certifications such as Lean Six Sigma or BPM are highly regarded

Why Deloitte?

At Deloitte, we focus our energy on interesting and impactful work. We're always learning, innovating and setting the standard; making a positive difference to our clients and our society. We put coaching at the heart of what we do, helping our people grow their careers in any direction - whether it be up, moving into something new, or even moving across the world.
We embrace diversity, equity and inclusion. We have a diverse collection of people from different backgrounds, with different experiences, gender identities, abilities and thinking styles. What binds us together is a shared commitment to value everyone's perspective and to cultivate inclusion; so that our work environment is a safe space we can all belong.
We value in-person connection with our clients and our colleagues. We offer several ways for you to work flexibly so that you can serve your clients, stay connected with your team, and manage your personal priorities.
We help you live and work well. To support your personal and professional life, we offer a range of perks and benefits, including retail discounts, wellbeing leave, paid volunteering days, twelve flexible working options, market-leading parental leave and return to work support package.

Next Steps

Sound like the sort of role for you? Apply now, we'd love to hear from you!

#LI-Hybrid

By applying for this job, you'll be assessed against the Deloitte Talent Standards. We've designed these standards so that you can grow in your career, and we can provide our clients with a consistent and exceptional Deloitte employee experience globally. The preferred candidate will be subject to background screening by Deloitte or by their external third-party provider.

An opportunity has arisen for an experienced quantitative professional to join a Credit Modelling team within a Counterparty Credit Risk (CCR) function, supporting a global commodities trading and financing business.

This role focuses on the development, enhancement and oversight of models used to measure counterparty credit exposures across trading portfolios. The successful candidate will work closely with risk, trading and credit teams to strengthen modelling frameworks and ensure alignment with regulatory expectations.

You will be part of a collaborative environment where quantitative expertise and strong stakeholder engagement are essential to delivering robust risk measurement and analysis.

Key Responsibilities
Enhance and maintain counterparty credit risk exposure models, ensuring they remain robust, accurate and aligned with internal risk standards and regulatory expectations.
Contribute to the design and development of new modelling methodologies and analytical tools used to measure counterparty exposures.
Calibrate risk factor evolution (RFE) parameters for newly traded instruments and assets not currently incorporated within existing systems.
Monitor model performance by developing processes and analytical tools to review outputs and identify areas for improvement.
Produce clear and insightful risk analysis and reporting for internal stakeholders and senior management.
Support the migration away from legacy modelling approaches, assisting with the onboarding of new products and asset classes into the primary simulation-based CCR framework.
Partner with front office trading teams and credit functions to respond to queries and provide expertise relating to CCR models and exposure calculations.

Skills and Experience
Advanced academic background in a quantitative discipline such as Mathematics, Statistics, Actuarial Science, Engineering or Computer Science.
At least 7 years' experience within a Quantitative role with exposure to the development or validation of counterparty credit risk or market risk models.
Technical proficiencies in Python, R, C++ or equivalent.

Execute pricing strategy

Manage yield and deal outcomes.

What you’ll bring to this role  

2+ years’ experience in evaluating pricing or commercial proposals in a B2B environment
Telco or ICT experience ideal but not essential
High performing individual with excellent financial modelling and analytical skills
High level of detail orientation and sound commercial acumen  
Strong interpersonal engagement skills and service orientation be naturally “hands on” with an ability to work efficiently and effectively as part of a small team
